Product Capability: Candidate Job Application Flow;Communications and Notifications
Severity: Tolerable, but Requires Some Manual Effort (5)
Functionality Requirement:
Template Editor: A rich-text editor allowing users to create and save predefined subject lines and email bodies.

Trigger Integration: The ability to select a specific template directly within the "Schedule Interview" workflow.

Default Settings: Option to set a "standard" template for specific stages to minimize clicks.
Headliner Feature:
Dynamic Communication Templates
A centralized library for creating, managing, and deploying reusable messaging templates for interview scheduling and candidate updates.
Pain point(s):
Operational Inefficiency: Recruiters waste significant time manually copy-pasting text from external documents into the platform for every interview.

Consistency Risk: High probability of "human error" (e.g., leaving the wrong name or old link in the body) due to manual editing.

Brand Fragmentation: Difficult to ensure a unified tone of voice and professional branding across the entire recruiting team without locked templates.

Competitive Disadvantage: The customer perceives our tool as higher-friction compared to Greenhouse, which offers a more automated communication flow.

These documents should be stored as distinct, structured document categories on the candidate and worker profile for easy visibility, reporting, and downstream use.
Headliner Feature: Structured Multi-Document Upload for Candidates
Industry Specific Gap Detail: Law Firms expect candidates to be able to seamlessly upload additional documents (transcripts, cover letters, writing samples).

Proposed Solution: Introduce native support for structured multi-document uploads within the core application process. Allow configurable document categories that surface clearly on the candidate and worker profile, ensuring easy access for recruiters and consistent storage for long-term recordkeeping.

Opportunity: QXO - cHCM, PRA, PLNW, USP, CAP, GPC, REC, LRN, CCB, TLO, HELP, JRNY, PEAK, HiredScore AI, PA
Severity: Tolerable, but Requires Some Manual Effort (5)

Pain point(s): The client prospect mentioned that they had previous Workday experience and disliked having to manually add candidates to talent pools themselves and wished that the system could auto-tag candidates for them as this was a tedious task for them.
Proposed Solution: Explained best practices as to why auto-tagging may not be as beneficial as one might think.  For example, they probably wouldn't want to tag candidates that are in the review stage that they haven't gotten to yet with something like a "not reviewed CSR's" tag versus those that have been interviewed but were still strong candidates like a "Silver Medalist CSRs" tag.  After explaining this they understood that having the control to properly label would ultimately be better than just having the system tag everyone the same tag that were not reviewed or rejected.

Severity: Significantly Contributed to Deal Loss (1)
CI Notes: eArcu has built in e-signature capabilities.
Functionality Requirement: Workday's ATS module needs native e-signature functionality that is included within the core Workday licensing or offered at a highly competitive, integrated price point, negating the need for separate, additional third-party contracts (e.g., DocuSign, Adobe) for standard e-signature workflows within the recruitment process. This will ensure Workday remains a cost-effective and competitive solution compared to alternative ATS providers
Headliner Feature: E-Signatures: Built-In, Not 3rd party
Pain point(s): The increased cost and reduced competitiveness of Workday's Recruiting due to the absence of included, native e-signature capabilities. Customers are forced to incur additional expenses for third-party integrations (like DocuSign or Adobe) to gain a feature that competitors offer as standard, making Workday a less attractive and less cost-effective solution.
Proposed Solution:
Developing native e-signature capabilities directly within Workday's Recruiting module. This functionality would be included as part of the core Workday licensing, eliminating the need for customers to purchase and integrate separate third-party e-signature solutions like DocuSign or Adobe.

This integrated approach will ensure Workday Recruiting offers a truly cost-effective and competitive solution that meets the essential recruitment needs of our customers without incurring additional, external expenses.
Gap Summary:
For this opp. the Head of TA said that their current ATS eArcu has e-signature capabilities for which they do not have to pay extra. At Workday we offer a 3rd party integration with DocuSign and Adobe, but the customer has to pay extra for that - on top of what they pay for Workday. THis meant that Workday was no longer cost effective when compared with their current solution.
They were not interested in the electronic Acknowledgment.

Severity: Significantly Contributed to Deal Loss (1)
CI Notes: eArcu has the ability to store documents e.g full legal job description and have them downloaded by external candidates.
Functionality Requirement: Ability to download attachements (e.g full legal job description) from the job posting/job advert.
Headliner Feature: Enhance Candidate Attraction: Allow Direct JD Downloads
Pain point(s):
Cumbersome Job Adverts Hinder Candidate Attraction

The current inability to directly attach full legal job descriptions (JDs) to job adverts forces our prospects to either lengthen the advert significantly to include all necessary information or rely on external links (corporate website/public cloud storage). Both solutions create a negative candidate experience that can negatively impact attraction. Lengthy adverts are visually overwhelming and deter quick review, while storing documents on a public storage service/company website increases maintenance. This ultimately leads to decreased applicant quality and quantity.
Proposed Solution: Empower external candidates with instant access to comprehensive job details by enabling the direct download of full legal job descriptions from external career site postings. This solution would eliminate the need for lengthy adverts or external links, and ultimately improves the candidate experience and attraction
Gap Summary:
For this opp. Optima, the Head of TA wanted the ability for candidates to be able to review the job advert and than download the full legal JR description.
We explained that their options would be to include the full description in the job posting or to store the document containing the JR description on the company corporate website/public cloud storage service. However the comment we received from the HEad of TA was "Not being able to attach the job description to the advert means adverts will need to be lengthened to include all info which can have a negative impact on attraction"

Severity: Risk of Deal Loss (3)

As of now, an external career site is allowed to register only one set of terms and conditions per country.

	In fact, this issue has caused some cases where customers claimed to cancel their purchases of the recruitment module.

Functionality Requirement: We used to be able to hide the "My Experience" and "My Information" sections of the application process. This allowed customers to be able to create an Evergreen req with only three sections to complete. This was removed in some previous release of Workday. Now they are forced to have a minimum of 4 sections which greatly increases the application time.
Headliner Feature: "Skinny" application process
Pain point(s): Can no longer have a "Skinny" application process
Proposed Solution: Add back the functionality to hide the "My Experience" and "My Information" sections of the Job Application Template.

Severity: Requires a 3rd Party Partner or Custom Solution (4)
Functionality Requirement: Perceived lack of co-development for a premier partner
Headliner Feature: Verizon has expressed frustration in confusion around Paradox partnership commitment, robust roadmap, and clarity.
Available Capability: Representative Capability is not in list
Pain point(s): See Additional detials.
Gap Summary:
from CSM Mara D: After our meeting at Verizon last week, we had some happy hour time with Cari Driscoll (SVP, HR) and Mike Bonomo (AVP, IT).  We were casually talking about some frustration that has been brewing on Mike’s team and one specific item is that there doesn’t seem to be real or effective collaboration happening from a roadmap perspective between Paradox and Workday, despite Workday recommending Paradox as a partner who could complement and solve for some things that WD can’t do.  Mike wanted us to join his team’s Paradox calls – which I do not think is appropriate but I do want to look into how at least generally we can raise awareness on behalf of this mutual customer.

WHILE THE CSM HAS LOOPED IN THE STRATEGIC CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT TEAM, THE FOLLOWING IS WHAT I PROVIDED TO THE CSM AND TEAM TO PREPARE THEM FOR POTENTIAL OBJECTIONS THAT COULD ARISE IN SUBSEQUENT CONVERSATIONS:

 
Sharing some thoughts from what I’ve seen in joint pursuits with Paradox in the field. Hope this helps.
 
While the collaboration between Workday and Paradox allows for surfacing Paradox's Olivia chatbot on Workday External Career Sites and Candidate Engagement Landing Pages, there are a few situations where the Paradox and Workday partnership could appear to be out of sync.
 
Workday External Career Site Bypass: Paradox's custom career sites (preferred by many large enterprise customers) replace Workday's External Career Site as the primary job posting location.
Candidate Engagement Incompatibility: Workday's Candidate Engagement Landing Pages are incompatible with custom front-end solutions, forcing customers to maintain a redundant "shadow" Workday External Career Site to use Workday’s Candidate Engagement functionality. This negates the value of the joint development effort to integrate the chatbot on Workday pages when custom career sites are used.

Competitive Overlap: Paradox offers a competing and robust Candidate Engagement product. Our leadership has commitment from Paradox to avoid direct competition with Workday CE in existing joint sales pursuits.

Workday SMS Limitation: Customers using Paradox cannot utilize Workday SMS for Workday Recruiting or CE campaign use cases.
Opt-in Discrepancy: SMS opt-in consent doesn't transfer from candidate to Worker when using a third-party (like Paradox) instead of Workday exclusively.
Example: We are releasing Preboarding with plans for SMS to help with communication of new hires. Candidates who have opted into Recruiting SMS notifications through Paradox today will not be automatically opted in to receive preboarding or onboarding notifications from Workday at the transition point.

Lack of future roadmap items: I haven’t seen anything Paradox related on the roadmap beyond what’s already been released. Additionally, roadmap items for Hiredscore plan to include Workday SMS and CE campaigns, which would be of null value for Paradox customers. Would love to hear about more, but this is all I’ve seen that’s been officially published.
